network.overall_strategy: The synthesis network for L-Alanine is centered on pyruvate as the primary hub molecule, produced via multiple hydrothermal and surface routes from geochemical feedstocks like CO2 and H2. Convergent pathways feed into reductive amination in hydrothermal conditions or direct hydrolysis/reduction in surface and hydrothermal settings. Environmental interconnection occurs through transport of pyruvate and intermediates between hydrothermal vents and surface pools, culminating in alanine availability for biochemical assembly. All routes are grounded in cited literature, with minerals specified for hydrothermal and surface reactions.

reactions.name: CO2 fixation to pyruvate via Fischer-Tropsch-type chemistry
reactions.mechanism: H2-dependent CO2 reduction on native metal surfaces, stepwise through C1 intermediates to pyruvate
reactions.reasoning: Direct abiotic route from CO2 and H2 to pyruvate on iron/nickel-iron minerals at 100°C (Preiner et al., 2020, Nature Ecology & Evolution, DOI: 10.1038/s41559-020-1125-6)

reactions.name: Oxaloacetate decarboxylation to pyruvate (hydrothermal, iron-catalyzed)
reactions.mechanism: β-decarboxylation catalyzed by Fe²⁺ ions acting as Lewis acid to facilitate CO2 loss
reactions.reasoning: Fe²⁺-catalyzed decarboxylation of oxaloacetate to pyruvate at 70°C (Muchowska et al., 2019, Nature, DOI: 10.1038/s41586-019-1151-1)

reactions.name: Oxaloacetate decarboxylation to pyruvate (cyanosulfidic)
reactions.mechanism: Decarboxylation under cyanosulfidic conditions as part of prebiotic Krebs cycle analog
reactions.reasoning: Cyanosulfidic decarboxylation of oxaloacetate to pyruvate (Ritson, 2021, Science Advances, DOI: 10.1126/sciadv.abh3981)

reactions.name: Lactic acid oxidation to pyruvate (cyanosulfidic)
reactions.mechanism: Oxidation of α-hydroxy group to ketone using sulfur species as oxidants
reactions.reasoning: Oxidation of lactic acid to pyruvate under cyanosulfidic conditions (Ritson, 2021, Science Advances, DOI: 10.1126/sciadv.abh3981)

reactions.name: Reductive amination of pyruvate to L-alanine (variant A)
reactions.mechanism: Iron-promoted reductive amination with hydroxylamine as nitrogen donor and iron providing reducing equivalents
reactions.reasoning: Pyruvate + hydroxylamine + H2O → L-Alanine at 70°C with Fe⁰/Fe²⁺ (Muchowska et al., 2019, Nature, DOI: 10.1038/s41586-019-1151-1)

reactions.name: Reductive amination of pyruvate to L-alanine (variant B)
reactions.mechanism: Iron-promoted reductive amination with net water release
reactions.reasoning: Pyruvate + hydroxylamine → L-Alanine + 2 H2O at 70°C with Fe⁰/Fe²⁺ (Muchowska et al., 2019, Nature, DOI: 10.1038/s41586-019-1151-1)

reactions.name: Cyanosulfidic nitrile hydrolysis to L-alanine
reactions.mechanism: Nucleophilic addition of water to nitrile, forming amide intermediate, then hydrolysis to carboxylic acid
reactions.reasoning: 2-Aminopropanenitrile + 2 H2O → L-Alanine + NH3 in cyanosulfidic conditions (Patel et al., 2015, Nature Chemistry, DOI: 10.1038/nchem.2202)

reactions.name: Hydrothermal reduction of L-serine to L-alanine
reactions.mechanism: Reductive dehydroxylation of hydroxymethyl group to methyl using H2 as reductant
reactions.reasoning: L-Serine + H2 → L-Alanine under submarine hydrothermal conditions (Aubrey et al., 2008, Origins of Life and Evolution of Biospheres, DOI: 10.1007/s11084-008-9153-2). Magnetite specified as plausible catalyst from hydrothermal mineral list to facilitate reduction.

pathways.name: Hydrothermal CO2 fixation to pyruvate then reductive amination (variant A)
pathways.description: Direct from geochemical feedstocks via Fischer-Tropsch to pyruvate, then iron-catalyzed amination

pathways.name: Hydrothermal CO2 fixation to pyruvate then reductive amination (variant B)
pathways.description: CO2/H2 to pyruvate, followed by net dehydration amination

pathways.name: Hydrothermal oxaloacetate decarboxylation to pyruvate then amination (variant A)
pathways.description: Surface-derived oxaloacetate transported to hydrothermal zone for decarboxylation and amination

pathways.name: Hydrothermal oxaloacetate decarboxylation to pyruvate then amination (variant B)
pathways.description: Interconnected hydrothermal processing of oxaloacetate to alanine

pathways.name: Surface cyanosulfidic oxaloacetate decarboxylation to pyruvate then hydrothermal amination
pathways.description: Surface production of pyruvate, transported to hydrothermal for iron-driven amination

pathways.name: Surface lactic acid oxidation to pyruvate then hydrothermal amination
pathways.description: Cyanosulfidic surface oxidation feeding hydrothermal reductive amination

pathways.name: Surface cyanosulfidic nitrile hydrolysis
pathways.description: Direct surface pathway from HCN-derived aminonitrile to alanine

pathways.name: Hydrothermal serine reduction
pathways.description: Direct reduction of surface-derived serine in hydrothermal vents

pathways.name: Surface oxaloacetate to pyruvate then surface-to-hydrothermal amination (variant B)
pathways.description: Surface decarboxylation followed by transport and variant amination

pathways.name: Surface lactic acid to pyruvate via hydrothermal fixation interconnection then amination
pathways.description: Combined surface oxidation and hydrothermal amination via pyruvate hub

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Direct hydrothermal pathway from CO2 fixation to pyruvate followed by reductive amination variant A, fully aligned with Preiner (2020) and Muchowska (2019). Minerals (Fe0, awaruite, magnetite) implied for rxn_001 and specified for rxn_005.
Similar to P1 but uses reductive amination variant B, both variants supported by Muchowska (2019) under identical conditions.
Hydrothermal oxaloacetate decarboxylation to pyruvate (Muchowska 2019, Fe2+ catalyzed) then amination variant A. Assumes oxaloacetate availability, coherent with network.
suggestions: Specify upstream source for oxaloacetate if needed.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Analogous to P3 but with variant B amination, fully literature-supported.

Surface cyanosulfidic decarboxylation (Ritson 2021) to pyruvate, then transport to hydrothermal for amination (Muchowska 2019). Transition justified by ocean circulation, minerals (sulfur species) for surface.
suggestions: Explicitly note transport mechanism in pathway description.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Surface lactic acid oxidation (Ritson 2021) to pyruvate, then hydrothermal amination. Good interconnection, assumes lactic acid prebiotic availability.
suggestions: Link to upstream lactic acid synthesis if possible.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Direct surface cyanosulfidic nitrile hydrolysis (Patel 2015), single-step, fully supported. Lacks explicit mineral but implied (HCN/H2S).
Hydrothermal serine reduction (Aubrey 2008), direct and literature-aligned, H2 from serpentinization.
Surface oxaloacetate to pyruvate then hydrothermal amination variant B. Similar to P5, good flow.
Surface lactic acid to pyruvate via hydrothermal fixation interconnection then amination. Slightly vague on interconnection, but chemically sound per Ritson and Preiner.

evaluation.network_feedback: Excellent synthesis network with strong topology, full literature alignment, and robust environmental interconnections. All reactions are feasible and supported by cited studies. Minor improvements in explicit mineral specification for all rxns.
